about.title: Hi!
about.content:
I am a Full-stack developer more experienced as a Front-end with JavaScript/TypeScript with extensive experience in React.js and Next.js. I build dynamic, responsive interfaces with a strong focus on performance and user experience.

 I’m a proactive, continuous learner passionate about turning ideas into real-world solutions. I thrive in collaborative environments and consistently contribute value through teamwork, ownership, and problem-solving. 

 My background in design gives me a keen eye for pixel-perfect implementation and a deep understanding of UI/UX principles. I excel at translating design visions into engaging, accessible digital experiences—applying mobile-first development, strong SEO practices, and accessibility standards from the ground up. 

 My expertise extends to the back-end, where I architect and maintain scalable solutions using Nest.js, Prisma ORM, and PostgreSQL. I am also proficient in the Web3 ecosystem, with hands-on experience interacting with smart contracts using WAGMI and querying on-chain data via Alchemy.

 My core focus is on delivering end-to-end technological solutions that prioritize efficiency, innovation, and a seamless user journey from database to interface.

content.name: Ervin Mamutov
content.role: Senior Software Engineer co-worker at KLKTN
content.comment:
Daniel is one of the most talented and dedicated people I’ve ever worked with. Watching him grow from a graphic designer into a highly skilled engineer in just three years was incredible. It really shows his discipline, hard work, and natural talent. 

 What truly sets Daniel apart is his approach to teamwork. He’s always the first to step up, no matter the task, and consistently delivers thoughtful, precise, and well-rounded solutions. He has a great sense for making sure his work is both user-friendly and robust. Beyond that, he brings so much positivity and energy to the team—it’s impossible to overstate how much better he makes the work environment. 

 Any team would be lucky to have Daniel. He’s not just talented; he’s the kind of teammate who makes everyone around him better.

content.name: Fabien Allanic
content.comment:
I had the pleasure to manage Daniel for a few years at KLKTN. 

 Daniel is not only talented and good at what he does, but he also embodies what I love to see in each engineer: being proactive with things, lead by example, willing to learn and grow, and is always up for a challenge. 

 He has delivered excellent work, and rose up to the occasion on many circumstances to fill any gap we had, and deliver high quality code while meeting our deadlines. 

 He is great to collaborate with, a fast learner, and was greatly appreciated by all of his teammates. 

 I highly recommend Daniel!

last_exp.title: Last Experience
last_exp.description: KLKTN as it began was music Web3 startup that pivoted to become SanFran Tokyo, an anime and manga Web3 company. Throughout this journey, I collaborated on various projects where I applied my **UI expertise** to streamline and polish frontend implementations. While focused on full-time **Frontend development**, I frequently contributed to **Backend** tasks when needed to unblock progress and enhance team efficiency.

projects.title: Weebox
projects.description: A highly interactive NFT store for anime and manga, built around a storytelling experience. I was responsible for the **Frontend** implementation of a Gacha machine feature, as well as all page **animations** and **structural development**.

projects.title: SFT Studios
projects.description: As the umbrella institution for all projects, SFT Studios required a robust online presence. I built its page from **scratch**, taking full **ownership** of the development, deployment, and maintenance.

projects.title: Anime ID
projects.description: Anime ID is a platform for anime and manga fans to connect and create an identity rooted in their fan assets. I contributed to the first version of the client dashboard, handling **Frontend** development while also delivering complete **Backend** features.

projects.title: Maru Project
projects.description: Maru was a campaign by the Japanese band Gesu no Kiwami Otome, created to share band news alongside the release of an exclusive new single. The single was transformed into NFT art featuring fragments of the songs, all showcased on a dedicated landing page. I contributed to the **majority** of the project, developing the NFT **animations**, **processing** NFT assets **building** the landing page, and **integrating** PayPal. Additionally, special NFTs included an exclusive physical LP featuring the NFT art and song.

projects.title: KLKTN
projects.description: KLKTN was a Kickstarter project that combined an institutional presence with an NFT store and showcase for K-pop and independent musicians. The platform featured a fresh design by a fashion-industry designer, giving it a strong and distinctive identity. I contributed by developing **Frontend** components and **automating** asset processing through batch scripts.

other_exp.title: Previous Experience
other_exp.description: Softcenter is a consolidated logistics management software company with a robust and mature solution, I enter to collaborate with new fresh ideas and company re-modernization.

projects.title: Software Dashboard
projects.description: Designed and Implement a software Dashboard screen with modern standard for better report consumption as way to provide better insights scenarios for clients for existing solution. The project has some technologies restrictions so **pure Javascript**, Html and CSS (SASS) were utilized to implement this project

projects.title: Marketing Tasks
projects.description: Worked also for Market department on planing helping re-shaping marketing strategies and measurements assisting with content planning and creation for better leads and sales process.

personal_project.title: Personal Project
personal_project.description: Espera Queue (project code name) is a personal WIP project that aims practice and also showcase, test and improve as full-stack developer, the test project is a solution to manage restaurants, bars and other types of business that needs to to manage waiting lines when it need at most, when business is running on max capacity and it needs all workforce to attend seated customers, Espera Queue automatically manages waiting lines

formations.institution: UTFPR - Universidade Tecnológica Federal do Paraná campus Londrina / Paraná Federal Technology University Londrina campus

footer.title: Daniel (Vichi) Ishigaki
footer.vichi:
Why Vichi? 

 Vichi is a nickname from early digital presence on games and chat servers, most friends and co-workers know me as Vichi, so I decided to use it as my domain and brand name for this portfolio.
